faqs.q: Can you notarize family law documents for women at CRDF Lynwood?
faqs.a: Yes. We frequently notarize child custody, guardianship, visitation, divorce, and support-related documents for women in custody at CRDF Lynwood when a notarized signature is required.

faqs.q: What information do you need to schedule a CRDF notary visit?
faqs.a: We need the inmate’s full legal name, booking number if available, a description of the documents that need to be notarized, and any deadlines or upcoming court dates.

faqs.q: Do you coordinate with attorneys for CRDF notary appointments?
faqs.a: Yes. We routinely coordinate with family law and criminal defense attorneys to ensure documents are ready to sign and meet court requirements before the notary visit at CRDF Lynwood.

faqs.q: How long does a CRDF Lynwood notary appointment take?
faqs.a: Plan for 1–2 hours including travel, security screening, wait time, and the actual signing. The notarization itself is quick, but jail security procedures add time.

faqs.q: Can you notarize real estate or financial documents at CRDF?
faqs.a: Yes. We can notarize deeds, loan documents, lease agreements, bank forms, and other financial paperwork, as long as the documents are prepared in advance and comply with California notary law.

faqs.q: Is MDC Los Angeles the same as Twin Towers or Men’s Central Jail?
faqs.a: No. Metropolitan Detention Center Los Angeles is a federal Bureau of Prisons facility that holds federal inmates, while Twin Towers and Men’s Central Jail are Los Angeles County jails.

faqs.q: Is the process the same as visiting a county jail?
faqs.a: No. MDC Los Angeles is a federal Bureau of Prisons facility with a more involved approval process for professional visitors. Federal visits require advance paperwork, attorney involvement, and approval that can take several business days.

faqs.q: Does the inmate’s attorney need to be involved?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes. Federal facilities typically require notary visits to be coordinated through or sponsored by the inmate’s attorney of record.

faqs.q: How far in advance should I schedule an MDC notary appointment?
faqs.a: We recommend contacting us at least one week before your deadline so we can complete Bureau of Prisons approval steps and schedule your appointment without rushing.

faqs.q: How do I confirm someone is at MDC Los Angeles?
faqs.a: You can search the Federal Bureau of Prisons inmate locator at bop.gov/inmateloc using the inmate’s name or register number, or contact MDC Los Angeles directly at (213) 485-0439.

faqs.q: Can you work with out-of-state attorneys or consulates?
faqs.a: Yes. We frequently coordinate MDC notary appointments with federal defense attorneys from other states and with consular officials to meet court and agency requirements.

faqs.q: How do I find someone's booking number at Men's Central Jail?
faqs.a: You can search the LA County Sheriff's Inmate Information Center online at app5.lasd.org/iic using the inmate's name and date of birth. The booking number helps confirm their location and coordinate the visit.

faqs.q: What if the inmate was just transferred to MCJ?
faqs.a: After a transfer, it can take 24–48 hours for an inmate to be fully processed and available for professional visits. We recommend confirming their status before scheduling if there has been a recent transfer.

faqs.q: Can family members bring documents to the jail notary appointment?
faqs.a: No — professional notary visits are separate from regular family visitation. You will need to get documents to us before the appointment, and we will bring them into the facility.

faqs.q: What ID does the inmate need for notarization at MCJ?
faqs.a: MCJ maintains booking records with photos that can be used for identification purposes. If the inmate has a valid California ID, driver's license, or passport on file, that can also be used.

faqs.q: How long does a Men's Central Jail notary appointment take?
faqs.a: Plan for 1–2 hours total including travel, security screening, wait time, and the actual signing. The notarization itself is quick, but jail procedures add time.

faqs.q: What if the inmate refuses to sign the documents?
faqs.a: A notary cannot force anyone to sign a document. If the inmate refuses or is unable to sign, the notarization cannot be completed. You may still be responsible for a trip fee in this situation.

faqs.q: Where is the James A. Musick Facility located?
faqs.a: The James A. Musick Facility is located at 13502 Musick Road, Irvine, CA 92618, in south Orange County near the junction of the I-5 and I-405 freeways.

faqs.q: What type of inmates are housed at Musick?
faqs.a: Musick primarily houses low-risk inmates, including those convicted of minor offenses and non-violent misdemeanors. It houses both male and female inmates and has a capacity of approximately 1,250 beds.

faqs.q: How do I confirm someone is at the Musick Facility?
faqs.a: You can search the Orange County Sheriff's Department inmate locator online or call the facility at (949) 855-2600 with the inmate's name and date of birth.

faqs.q: What ID does the inmate need for notarization at Musick?
faqs.a: The facility maintains booking records with photos that can be used for identification. Acceptable government-issued IDs include a California driver's license, California ID card, U.S. passport, alien registration card, or military ID, when available in the inmate's records.

faqs.q: Is the visiting environment different from other jails?
faqs.a: Yes. Musick is a minimum-security facility, so the visiting environment is generally more relaxed than at maximum-security jails. Visits may take place at benches rather than behind glass, and professional visitors often have more flexible access.

faqs.q: How long should I plan for a Musick notary appointment?
faqs.a: Plan for 2 to 3 hours total including travel from Los Angeles to south Irvine, check-in, wait time, and the signing. The notarization itself is quick, but the distance from LA adds significant travel time.

faqs.a: The Larry D. Smith Correctional Facility is located at 1627 S Hargrave Street, Banning, CA 92220, near the I-10 freeway in the San Gorgonio Pass area, approximately 1.5 hours from Los Angeles.

faqs.q: How do I confirm someone is at Larry D. Smith Correctional Facility?
faqs.a: You can search the Riverside County Sheriff's inmate locator online or call the facility at (951) 922-7300 with the inmate's name and date of birth to confirm they are housed at Smith Correctional.

faqs.a: You can call (951) 922-7540 between 9:00 AM and 5:00 PM daily to schedule visits, and visits must be scheduled at least one day in advance. For attorney or professional visits, the facility is contacted at (951) 922-7300 to coordinate directly.

faqs.a: Visitor check-in begins 60 minutes before the scheduled visit and closes 20 minutes before the start time. Visitors who arrive during the final 20 minutes before the visit will not be allowed to check in, so arriving early is essential.

faqs.q: What ID does the inmate need for notarization at Smith Correctional?
faqs.a: The facility maintains booking records that can be used for identification. If the inmate has a valid California ID, driver's license, or passport on file, that can also be used. ID options are confirmed with the facility before the appointment.

faqs.q: How long should I plan for a Larry D. Smith Correctional notary appointment?
faqs.a: Plan for 4 to 5 hours total including travel from Los Angeles (about 1.5 hours each way), security screening, wait time, and the signing. The notarization itself is quick, but travel and facility procedures add significant time.

faqs.a: The Robert Presley Detention Center serves as one of the primary booking and detention facilities for Riverside County, handling initial booking, pre-trial detention, and court appearances for the downtown Riverside courthouse area.

faqs.a: The facility maintains booking records that can be used for identification. If the inmate has a valid California ID, driver's license, or passport on file, that can also be used. ID options are confirmed with the facility before the appointment.

faqs.a: RPDC is in downtown Riverside. Metered parking is available on surrounding streets, and two public parking structures are at the corner of Lemon and 12th Street. Extra time should be allowed for parking and walking to the facility.

faqs.a: The Central Detention Center serves primarily as a booking and short-term housing facility for San Bernardino County. faqs.a: The facility maintains booking records with photos that can be used for identification. If the inmate has a valid California ID, driver's license, or passport on file, that can also be used. ID options are confirmed with the facility before the appointment.

faqs.a: Public visiting hours at WVDC are Wednesday through Saturday, 8:30 AM to 7:00 PM, by appointment only. Visits must be scheduled at least one day in advance by calling (909) 887-0364 or using the online scheduling system. Attorneys may visit any day of the week.

faqs.q: How is the inmate's identity verified for notarization at WVDC?
faqs.a: The facility maintains booking records with photos that can be used for identification. If the inmate has a valid California ID, driver's license, or passport on file, that can also be used. Identification options are confirmed with the facility before the appointment.

faqs.q: How long should I plan for a West Valley Detention Center notary appointment?
faqs.a: You should plan for 2 to 3 hours total including travel from Los Angeles to Rancho Cucamonga, security screening, wait time, and the signing. The notarization itself is quick, but travel and jail procedures add time.

faqs.q: Where is the Santa Barbara County Main Jail located?
faqs.a: The Santa Barbara County Main Jail is located at 4436 Calle Real, Santa Barbara, CA 93110. It is the county's primary jail and houses male and female inmates awaiting trial, sentencing, or serving county jail sentences.

faqs.q: Do mobile notaries work at the Santa Barbara Main Jail?
faqs.a: Yes. Independent mobile notaries regularly provide services at the Santa Barbara County Main Jail. The notary checks in with custody staff, is escorted to a non-contact visiting area, and completes the notarization with the inmate.

faqs.q: How is the inmate's identity verified for notarization at the Main Jail?
faqs.a: Custody staff maintain booking records with photos and identifying information that can be used to confirm the inmate's identity. If the inmate has a valid government-issued ID on file, that can also be used. ID options are confirmed with the facility before the appointment.

faqs.q: How long should I plan for a Santa Barbara Main Jail notary appointment?
faqs.a: Plan for 3 to 5 hours total, including travel from Los Angeles, check-in with custody staff, escort to and from the visiting area, wait time, and the signing itself. The notarization portion is quick, but facility procedures and distance add time.

faqs.q: Is the Northern Branch Jail the same as the Main Jail?
faqs.a: No. The Main Jail on Calle Real in Santa Barbara and the Northern Branch Jail on Black Road in Santa Maria are separate facilities about 65 miles apart. Both are operated by the Santa Barbara County Sheriff's Office, so it is important to confirm which facility the inmate is housed at before scheduling.

faqs.q: How do I confirm someone is at the Santa Barbara County Main Jail?
faqs.a: You can search the Santa Barbara County Sheriff's Office inmate locator online or call the Main Jail at (805) 681-4260 with the inmate's name and date of birth.

faqs.q: Where is the Northern Branch Jail located?
faqs.a: The Northern Branch Jail is located at 2301 Black Road, Santa Maria, CA 93455. It is a 376-bed Santa Barbara County facility that houses male and female inmates in medium and high-security housing units.

faqs.q: Can a mobile notary visit the Northern Branch Jail?
faqs.a: Yes. Independent mobile notaries can provide services at the Northern Branch Jail. The notary coordinates with the watch commander, checks in with custody staff, and completes the notarization during a professional visit.

faqs.q: What are the visiting hours and scheduling rules at Northern Branch?
faqs.a: The Northern Branch Jail offers visits Sunday through Saturday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Visits are scheduled through the ViaPath online registration system. Appointments start on the hour, and visitors should check in 15 to 30 minutes before their scheduled time.

faqs.q: How many visits are inmates allowed at Northern Branch Jail?
faqs.a: Inmates at the Northern Branch Jail are generally allowed up to four visits per week. All visitors must be on the inmate's approved visitor list and must check in with the same photo ID used to register.

faqs.q: Is the Northern Branch Jail the same as the Santa Barbara County Main Jail?
faqs.a: No. The Northern Branch Jail in Santa Maria and the Main Jail on Calle Real in Santa Barbara are separate facilities about 65 miles apart. Both are operated by the Santa Barbara County Sheriff's Office, and many social visits for county inmates are handled at the Northern Branch.

faqs.q: How long should I plan for a Northern Branch Jail notary appointment?
faqs.a: Plan for 5 to 7 hours total, including travel from Los Angeles to Santa Maria (approximately 2.5 hours each way), check-in with custody staff, wait time, and the signing itself. The notarization portion is quick, but the distance adds significant travel time.

faqs.q: How do I confirm someone is at the Northern Branch Jail?
faqs.a: You can search the Santa Barbara County Sheriff's Office inmate locator online or call the Northern Branch Jail at (805) 554-3100 with the inmate's name and date of birth.

faqs.q: Where is East Valley Jail located?
faqs.a: East Valley Jail is located at 2101 E Olsen Rd, Thousand Oaks, CA 91360. It is approximately 45 minutes from Los Angeles via US-101.

faqs.q: How do I confirm someone is housed at East Valley Jail?
faqs.a: You can search the Ventura County Sheriff's inmate locator online or call (805) 371-8163. If the inmate is not listed at East Valley, they may be at Ventura County Main Jail or Todd Road Jail.

faqs.q: What are the visiting days and policies at East Valley Jail?
faqs.a: Visiting schedules at East Valley Jail are set by the Ventura County Sheriff's Office and may differ from other county facilities. Contact the facility or call us to confirm current visiting days, visit length, and visitor limits.

faqs.q: What can I bring into East Valley Jail during a visit?
faqs.a: Only your personal key ring, photo ID, and one baby bottle or blanket if bringing an infant. No cell phones, wallets, purses, backpacks, food, beverages, cameras, or reading materials are allowed. The facility will not hold items and there are no storage lockers.

faqs.q: How is East Valley Jail different from Ventura County Main Jail or Todd Road Jail?
faqs.a: East Valley Jail is a minimum-security facility that houses lower-classification inmates and those nearing release. Ventura County Main Jail in Ventura primarily handles booking and pre-trial detainees, while Todd Road Jail in Santa Paula primarily houses sentenced inmates in longer-term custody.

faqs.q: How long should I plan for an East Valley Jail notary appointment?
faqs.a: Plan for 2 to 3 hours total including travel from Los Angeles (approximately 45 minutes each way via US-101), security screening, wait time, and the signing.

faqs.q: Where is Ventura County Main Jail located?
faqs.a: The Ventura County Main Jail, also known as the Pre-Trial Detention Facility, is located at 800 S Victoria Ave, Ventura, CA 93009. It is approximately 65 minutes from Los Angeles via US-101.

faqs.q: How do I confirm someone is housed at Ventura County Main Jail?
faqs.a: You can search the Ventura County Sheriff's inmate locator online or call (805) 654-3335. Be sure to confirm whether the inmate is at the Main Jail or at Todd Road Jail in Santa Paula.

faqs.q: What are the visiting days and sessions at Ventura County Main Jail?
faqs.a: Visiting days are Wednesday, Thursday, Saturday, and Sunday. Sessions are typically morning (8:30 AM – 11:30 AM), afternoon (1:30 PM – 4:30 PM), and evening (7:00 PM – 9:00 PM), assigned by housing unit. Check-in starts 1 hour before each session and closes 30 minutes before the session ends.

faqs.q: What can I bring into Ventura County Main Jail during a visit?
faqs.a: Only your personal key ring, photo ID, and one baby bottle or blanket if bringing an infant. No cell phones, wallets, purses, backpacks, food, beverages, cameras, or reading materials are allowed. The facility will not hold items and there are no storage lockers.

faqs.q: What is the difference between Ventura County Main Jail and Todd Road Jail?
faqs.a: The Main Jail (Pre-Trial Detention Facility) in Ventura primarily houses pre-trial detainees and handles initial booking. Todd Road Jail in Santa Paula primarily houses sentenced inmates and those in longer-term custody. They are separate facilities with different addresses and visiting schedules.

faqs.q: How long should I plan for a Ventura County Main Jail notary appointment?
faqs.a: Plan for 3 to 4 hours total including travel from Los Angeles, security screening, wait time, and the signing. The drive from Los Angeles to Ventura County Main Jail is approximately 65 minutes each way via US-101.

faqs.q: Where is Todd Road Jail located?
faqs.a: Todd Road Jail is located at 600 Todd Road, Santa Paula, CA 93060. It is approximately 70 minutes from Los Angeles via US-101.

faqs.q: How do I confirm someone is housed at Todd Road Jail?
faqs.a: You can search the Ventura County Sheriff's inmate locator online or call (805) 654-3335. Be sure to confirm whether the inmate is at Todd Road or the Main Jail in Ventura, as they are separate facilities.

faqs.q: What are the visiting days and check-in rules at Todd Road?
faqs.a: Visiting days are Wednesday, Thursday, Saturday, and Sunday. Each visit is 30 minutes, with two visits per inmate per week. Check-in starts 1 hour before and closes 30 minutes before the session ends.

faqs.q: What can I bring into Todd Road Jail during a visit?
faqs.a: Only your personal key ring, photo ID, and one baby bottle or blanket if bringing an infant. No cell phones, wallets, purses, backpacks, food, beverages, cameras, or reading materials are allowed. The facility will not hold items and there are no storage lockers.

faqs.q: What is the difference between Todd Road Jail and the Ventura County Main Jail?
faqs.a: The Main Jail in downtown Ventura primarily houses pre-trial detainees and handles initial booking. Todd Road in Santa Paula primarily houses sentenced inmates and those in longer-term custody, with different visiting schedules and housing units.

faqs.q: How long should I plan for a Todd Road notary appointment?
faqs.a: Plan for 3 to 4 hours total including travel from Los Angeles, security screening, wait time, and the signing. Todd Road is approximately 70 minutes each way via US-101.
